---
title: Stats
description: Track hit/miss ratio and latency percentiles via cache.stats().
icon: ChartBar
---

Exposes a `cache.stats()` method that returns hit/miss counts, hit rate, and latency percentiles for `get` and `set` operations.

## Setup

```ts
import { statsPlugin } from "@justwant/cache/plugins/stats";
import { createCache } from "@justwant/cache";
import { memoryAdapter } from "@justwant/cache/adapters/memory";

const cache = createCache({
  adapter: memoryAdapter(),
  plugins: [statsPlugin()],
});
```

## Reading stats

```ts
const stats = cache.stats?.();
```

`cache.stats` is `undefined` when the plugin is not registered, so the optional chain (`?.`) is the safe way to call it.

## Return shape

```ts
{
  hits: number;
  misses: number;
  hitRate: number;       // hits / (hits + misses), 0 when no requests yet
  sets: number;
  deletes: number;
  errors: number;
  latency: {
    get: { p50: number; p95: number; p99: number };  // milliseconds
    set: { p50: number; p95: number; p99: number };
  };
}
```

## Notes

- Latencies are computed from all operations since the cache instance was created — there is no rolling window.
- Errors incremented on any thrown exception in `get`, `set`, or `delete`.
- `statsPlugin` takes no options.
